Metadata.title: Metal 3D Printing
Metadata.description: At Metal 3D Printing, we provide metal 3D printing services. We quote your project free of charge and manufacture it in record time using DMLS technology. We can produce your product, post-process it, and even package it with your logo.

Hero.title: Metal 3D printing
Hero.subtitle: Get a free quote. Record-time manufacturing with high-precision additive technology.

About.title: Metal 3D Printing
About.body:
Metal 3D Printing is a division of Bioprotece S.A., focused on advanced additive manufacturing and precision machining solutions for the Argentine and Latin American industry.

From our facility in Villa Ballester, Buenos Aires, we integrate engineering, innovation, and cutting-edge technology to produce high-performance metal parts used across industrial, medical, energy, automotive, and aerospace sectors.
About.visionBody: Provide advanced additive manufacturing and precision machining solutions, delivering reliable high-performance metal parts manufactured under international quality standards.
About.missionBody: Be the leading reference in Latin America for innovation and advanced metal manufacturing, enabling customers to optimize lead times, reduce costs, and boost competitiveness through additive technologies.

Services.title: Metal additive manufacturing + precision machining in one facility
Services.body: At Metal 3D Printing we help industries reduce downtime, avoid production stops and accelerate the development of critical parts through metal additive manufacturing and precision machining in the same plant.
Services.subTitle: Three key needs:
Services.subBody: our services solve real operational and supply challenges.
Services.needsBody: Our services address three core needs across industrial operations:

needsCards.title: Urgent spare parts for production lines
needsCards.body:
We manufacture critical metal parts when the original spare is unavailable, discontinued, 
or has long lead times. Metal 3D printing delivers functional components in hours or days,
preventing operational losses.

needsCards.title: Complex parts that CNC cannot produce
needsCards.body:
We create internal geometries, hidden channels, organic curves,
and lightweight structures not feasible with traditional methods—while maintaining
strength and dimensional accuracy.

needsCards.title: Functional prototypes and short runs without molds
needsCards.body:
We produce titanium or steel parts without tooling, enabling faster validation, 
 rapid iteration, and short-run manufacturing with significantly reduced lead times.

Services.processBody: We combine engineering, metal 3D printing and post-processing to deliver parts ready to use.
Services.processHint: We can tailor the workflow to your material, tolerances and quality requirements.

processCards.title: Engineering
processCards.body:
We support your design, validate
STL dimensions and develop test pieces prior to the final 
 production print.

processCards.title: 3D Printing
processCards.body:
We operate three state-of-the-art DMLS printers and provide 
 material certificates
upon request.

processCards.title: Post-Processing
processCards.body:
In-house finishing options: polishing,
laser logo engraving, anodizing for color, and even packaging with your brand.

Services.industriesBody: Examples of sectors where metal additive manufacturing delivers immediate value.

sectors.title: OIL & GAS
sectors.body: Corrosion-resistant parts, complex geometries and critical spares to keep operations running.

sectors.title: AUTOMOTIVE / MACHINERY
sectors.body: Fixtures, validation parts and functional components to speed up iterations and reduce stops.

sectors.title: CHEMICAL / FOOD / PHARMA
sectors.body: Sanitary parts and critical line spares with a focus on quality, precision and process compatibility.

sectors.title: AEROSPACE / DRONES / TECHNOLOGY
sectors.body: Lightweight structures, optimized supports and metal housings for sensors and systems.

Work.title: Work
Work.subtitle: Real projects produced in record time using a wide range of biocompatible materials

Faq.title: Frequently asked questions
Faq.subtitle: Quick answers to the most common questions about metal 3D printing.
Faq.placeholder: Select a question to see the answer.

items.q: What is the maximum print area size?
items.a: Our largest 3D printers have a 26 cm × 26 cm build area, with a maximum height of 32 cm.

items.q: Which materials can you work with?
items.a:
We can use steel powders such as stainless steel 316L, medical-grade steel, and maraging steel.

Our strength is Grade 5 titanium: corrosion-resistant, stable at high temperatures without deformation, capable of withstanding high loads, and highly biocompatible.

In addition, our printers are compatible with aluminum, cobalt-chrome, copper alloys, and nickel alloys.

items.q: What tolerances and minimum dimensions can you offer?
items.a:
The minimum thickness we recommend manufacturing is 1 mm.

Dimensional tolerance: ±0.1 mm for dimensions under 100 mm. For larger dimensions, a rule of ±0.1% of nominal size is typically applied.
Minimum wall thickness: 0.3 mm (depends on the material).
Minimum printable hole diameter: generally 0.5 mm as a reliable minimum.
Surface roughness: Ra ~ 8–15 µm as-printed (depends on layer height).
Flatness: ±0.2 mm.

items.q: What surface finish can you achieve with this process?
items.a: Surface finish depends on the customer’s requirements. We can deliver three options: porous, matte polish, or mirror-bright polish.

items.q: Does the customer need to provide the part engineering?
items.a:
We always recommend that the customer provides the engineering/design as they want it, so we can define requirements, quality controls, parameters, etc.

However, we have an on-site engineering team to advise the customer throughout the project.
